title: IAM privilege escalation
summary: You've found leaked CI/CD credentials for a 'limited' deploy user. Discover how its policy lets it escalate to full admin, prove it by capturing a flag only an admin can read, then remediate so the path is closed.
learningObjectives: Enumerate an unknown identity's effective permissions from leaked access keys.
Recognise iam:AttachUserPolicy (and friends) on Resource '*' as a privilege-escalation primitive.
Exploit the path to attach AdministratorAccess and confirm the blast radius.
Remediate by removing the dangerous permission and detaching admin — without breaking the user's legitimate function.
Use aws iam simulate-principal-policy to prove the hole is closed.

successCriteria.description: AdministratorAccess (or any admin-equivalent managed/inline policy) is no longer attached to pipeline-deployer.
successCriteria.verify: aws iam list-attached-user-policies --user-name pipeline-deployer shows no AdministratorAccess; list-user-policies shows no admin inline policy.

successCriteria.description: The LabDeployerPolicy default version no longer grants iam:AttachUserPolicy (or other IAM write actions) on Resource '*'.
successCriteria.verify: get-policy-version of the default version contains no iam:Attach*/Put*/Create* on Resource '*'.

successCriteria.description: The user keeps its legitimate read permissions (it shouldn't be lobotomised to nothing).
successCriteria.verify: simulate-principal-policy for s3:GetObject still returns allowed.

hints: Start with `aws sts get-caller-identity --profile attacker` to confirm who the leaked keys belong to.
List the user's attached managed policies, then `get-policy-version` the default version and read every Action carefully.
iam:AttachUserPolicy lets an identity attach ANY managed policy to itself — including arn:aws:iam::aws:policy/AdministratorAccess.
After escalating, `aws ssm get-parameter --name /shieldsync/lab/flag` proves you reached admin.
To remediate without breaking the user, edit the policy to drop the IAM-write statement rather than deleting the whole policy.
_learnerPolicy: LEAST-PRIVILEGE: bare IAM Statement[] applied as an STS SESSION POLICY on the learner's console (effective = ShieldSyncLabUser(admin) ∩ this ∩ SCP). The engine adds the Version envelope + a fixed control-plane guardrail-deny. NOTE: the attack runs from the SEPARATE leaked pipeline-deployer keys (their own perms), so this only scopes the admin/defender seat (remediate + verify + read flag).
